message:
perf(core): optimize streaming renderer hot path and session serialization

Two targeted performance improvements:

view.rs — Streaming render hot path:
- Rewrite normalize_raw_terminal_newlines() to use memchr byte-scanning
  instead of char-by-char iteration, avoiding UTF-8 decode overhead on
  every view() call. This is the innermost loop of the terminal render
  pipeline.
- Refactor streaming markdown rendering: replace render_streaming_plaintext
  with append_streaming_plaintext_to_output that writes directly into the
  output buffer (eliminating an intermediate String allocation), and rename
  render_streaming_markdown to render_streaming_markdown_with_glamour. The
  caller now checks streaming_needs_markdown_renderer() first and takes the
  fast plaintext path when possible, avoiding Glamour subprocess overhead
  for plain text content.
- Add test normalize_raw_terminal_newlines_preserves_utf8_content.

session.rs — Serialization efficiency:
- Buffer pre-allocation for JSONL serialization now scales based on
  observed average on-disk entry size (clamped 512..256K) instead of a
  fixed 512-byte estimate, reducing reallocation churn for sessions with
  large entries.
- Simplify ensure_entry_ids(): remove redundant call since
  rebuild_all_caches() already runs finalize_loaded_entries() which
  fills missing IDs.
- Increase JSONL_PARSE_BATCH_SIZE from 2048 to 8192 for better
  throughput on large session files.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
